name: French Apple Slices
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, cut shortening into flour and salt until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
2. In a separate bowl, beat eggs slightly, then add water and lemon juice. Blend well.
3. Add the egg mixture to the flour and shortening mix. Stir only until a soft dough forms.
4. Roll 1/2 of the dough about 1/8 inch thick and place it in a greased 7x12 or 13x9 pan.
5. Arrange apples on the crust, rolling up one side.
6. Place another rolled-up dough over the apples to form the top crust. Cut several slits in the top crust.
7. Seal the edges of the crust together and bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 1 hour.
8. Peel and slice the apples thinly.
9. In a separate bowl, combine sugar, salt, flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and raisins or currants (if using).
10. Add the apple slices to the dry mixture and toss until the apples are evenly coated.
11. Place the coated apples into the prepared crust.
12. Before serving, mix the frosting ingredients in a bowl until smooth.
13. Drizzle the frosting over the cooled tart.
14. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator or freezer for extended storage. This recipe freezes well and can be enjoyed throughout the year.
This classic French apple tart is a delightful treat that pairs perfectly with ice cream. It's a beloved dish passed down through generations, having been made by my mother-in-law every fall. The apples are baked until tender, then topped with a sweet sugar mixture before being rolled up in pastry dough and sliced into individual portions. The result is a beautifully layered tart that can be enjoyed warm or at room temperature, dusted with powdered sugar for added sweetness.
